title: Prompt-time marker does not block trusted mutations

findingDetails.evidence: Prompt-time env vars are only folded into defaultWarmOnly() at lines 353-361. The mutation guard at lines 659-664 allows advisor_rebuild, skill_graph_scan, and apply-mode skill_graph_propagate_enhances whenever trusted is true, and runSkillAdvisorCli invokes the validated tool at lines 1289-1296. The spec safety claim at .opencode/specs/system-spec-kit/027-xce-research-based-refinement/before-vs-after.md:375 says these mutations are blocked from prompt-time paths entirely.
findingDetails.why: A prompt-time hook process with a warm daemon and --trusted or MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_CLI_TRUSTED=1 can still execute shared-state mutations. That makes the prompt-time safety invariant rely on every wrapper hardcoding only read tools instead of being enforced by the front-door.
findingDetails.recommendation: Carry a separate promptTime boolean out of parseCliArgs/default env detection and reject mutation tools before callTool when promptTime is true, regardless of --trusted or trusted env. Add regression tests for OPENCODE_PROMPT_TIME=1 with advisor_rebuild, skill_graph_scan, and apply-mode skill_graph_propagate_enhances.

title: Inline false still enables trusted mode

findingDetails.evidence: The parser derives rawFlag by stripping any =value at line 398, then the trusted/maintainer branch at lines 428-430 sets trusted=true without checking whether the token was --trusted=false, --trusted=0, or another inline value.
findingDetails.why: Safety wrappers or generated CLI calls that serialize booleans as --trusted=false will unexpectedly grant mutation authority. This is especially risky because trusted mode gates shared-state mutation tools.
findingDetails.recommendation: Reject inline values for --trusted/--maintainer, or parse --trusted=true|false with the same boolean parser used for --warm-only. Add tests proving --trusted=false and --trusted=0 do not satisfy the mutation gate.
